messages.text:
<@U06F97BPQJU> Per our discussion today we're going to do the following:
1. Prepare a document that includes the economics, application process, safety/green benefit, sampling size, production size, etc. This is here: <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1NCHAynS-GiivbVPR1kF-ujCAldz5QZ3cmfPU10OpGZQ/edit?pli=1>
    a. Please note that I've made ballpark estimates of the cost based on what I hope are conservative guesses. Austin is going to work on getting more accurate pricing. CHXMA is our most likely wild card, but I'm hopeful I'm in the right ballpark. 
2. We're going to do a full cure experiment this week and see if that influences our formulation at all. It might shift it to a little bit higher bio content. Once we have those results, we'll update the document, and also, we'll take out the individual component cost for the monomer system and aggregate them together so that Polyset has a more simplified cost breakdown (if you wish to provide that at all). 
3. Setting up a time to discuss. Do we want to try to put something on the books for next week?
Michael, once I look at the docs and you have a chance evaluate the cure, I’ll contact Nil at Polyset…probably mid next week. Thanks for your help.
